creator: remko@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->closed

This is a feature and not a bug: FreeBSD Uses a modified Biba/MLS model 
which allows subjects (or processes) to not only have an effective label 
but also a label range, which gives the privilege to float between the 
subject label and object labels between endpoints on the range.  If your 
process is labeled biba/8(low-high), then "8" is the current effective 
label, "low" is the low end of its range (as low as the label can be 
floated on the process or an object it's relabeling), and "high" is the 
high end of the range. So a process with that label can relabel any 
object in the system. If you set the label to biba/low(low-low), then 
the process wouldn't be authorized to relabel since its effective, 
upper, and lower bounds are all identical.  This information is provided 
by Robert Watson, thanks for that!
